MILO_OFFICIAL_SCRIPT_v1

seed_id: seed-107
edit_level: polish
script:
  setup: "My girlfriend just got a job at the zoo."
  punchline: "I'm going to propose — she's a keeper."
  tag: ~
changes_made: Cut 'This girl I've been dating' and replaced with 'My girlfriend' — cleaner, drops one indirect layer, and makes the narrator's emotional investment clearer which sharpens the romantic stakes before the punchline. Cut 'After all' — it's filler that softens the landing. Merged the proposal announcement and the punchline onto one card separated by an em-dash, which creates the micro-beat the joke needs without a full sentence break. 'She's a keeper' does double duty as zoo terminology and relationship approval — the delivery should land both meanings simultaneously.
caption_notes: Two cards. Setup: "My girlfriend just got a job at the zoo." Punchline: "I'm going to propose — she's a keeper." The em-dash inside the punchline card is intentional — it gives the viewer a half-beat to process 'propose' before 'keeper' drops.
timing_notes: Setup warm, genuine — not playing for laughs yet. 0.4s pause. Punchline delivered with sincere romantic confidence, not a wink. The joke is funnier if the narrator genuinely believes this is a romantic thing to say.
tts_notes: ~
quality_flag: solid

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v1

seed_id: seed-107
concepts_count: 2
casting_note: No RILEY_CASTING_PLAN provided; Iris assigned casting inline.

Cast assignments:
1. Jerry Gold — Boyfriend / narrator (Classic Dad persona). Tells the story with warm sincerity. He genuinely thinks "she's a keeper" is romantic. The pun is accidental.
2. Zoe Sparks — Girlfriend / zoo employee (Goofball Neighbor persona). Appears at the zoo in keeper uniform. Her natural enthusiasm makes the zoo job feel genuine.

---

## Concept A — "The Zoo Proposal"
Style: Zoo setting, Jerry nervously preparing to propose while Zoe works with animals
Duration: 9.0s — Needs time for the romantic setup before the pun deflates it.

Master video prompt:
"Outdoor zoo enclosure area, cartoon 2.5D illustration, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses) in a khaki zookeeper uniform with a name badge, standing near a fence with cartoon animals visible (giraffe neck, elephant trunk). Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) standing nearby, nervously holding a small ring box behind his back. Warm sunny outdoor lighting, green foliage. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.2s): HOOK — The Zoo
A bright, sunny outdoor zoo scene. Green trees and bushes frame a pathway along a large animal enclosure with a wooden fence. Behind the fence, a cartoon giraffe's long neck and head poke up, gently chewing leaves. A sign on the fence reads "GIRAFFE HABITAT." Standing near the fence, facing into the enclosure with her back partially to the viewer, is Zoe Sparks — a young woman with orange-magenta two-tone hair pulled into a ponytail, teal glasses, wearing a khaki zookeeper uniform (short-sleeved shirt, cargo pants, work boots) with a name badge that reads "ZOE." She holds a clipboard and looks up at the giraffe with genuine delight. In the foreground, partially hidden behind a bush, Jerry Gold lurks — a middle-aged man with brown hair, handlebar mustache, teal jacket — holding something small behind his back and looking nervous. Warm sunshine, blue sky.

Scene 2 (2.2–5.0s): SETUP — The News
Same zoo pathway. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) has stepped out from behind the bush and now faces the viewer directly, one hand still behind his back (hiding the ring box). His expression is warm and excited — he's telling the viewer his plan. Behind him, Zoe Sparks in her zookeeper uniform (orange-magenta ponytail, teal glasses, khaki outfit) leans over the fence feeding the giraffe, unaware of what's about to happen. A cartoon elephant trunk is visible reaching over the fence from an adjacent enclosure, also trying to get food. The zoo is alive and colorful. Jerry looks like a man in love with a plan.
Caption: "My girlfriend just got a job at the zoo."

Scene 3 (5.0–5.4s): THE BEAT
Same zoo path, closer framing on Jerry Gold. His hidden hand shifts behind his back — the small velvet ring box is barely visible behind his hip. His expression changes from warm to determined. He's ready. He's about to explain why this is the moment.

Scene 4 (5.4–9.0s): PUNCHLINE — The Reason
Same zoo path. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) has brought the ring box forward, holding it up near his chest — small, dark velvet, unopened. His other hand gestures toward Zoe behind him in a presenting motion. His expression is completely sincere romantic conviction — this is genuine, this is his reason. Behind him, Zoe Sparks in her zookeeper uniform (orange-magenta ponytail, teal glasses, khaki outfit) has turned away from the giraffe enclosure and is now looking toward Jerry with a surprised, touched expression — hand over her mouth, clipboard dropped to her side. She doesn't know about the ring box yet but she can sense something. The giraffe behind the fence has lowered its head to peer at the scene with curious cartoon eyes. Even the elephant trunk has paused. The whole zoo is watching. Jerry sees none of this — he's looking at the viewer, delivering his line with total sincerity.
Caption: "I'm going to propose — she's a keeper."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.2s no text | 2.2–5.0s setup | 5.4–9.0s punchline
Voice: Jerry warm and genuine throughout — this is a romantic story, not a comedy bit (to him). 0.4s pause. Punchline delivered with sincere confidence. "Keeper" should land both meanings simultaneously. No wink.
Risk: LOW. Zoo setting is visual-rich but recognizable. Animals may render imperfectly — cartoon style helps. Fallback: simplify to Jerry in front of a zoo entrance sign (no visible animals), holding ring box, Zoe visible through the gate in uniform.
Jerry hook: "The zoo is inherently visual, the ring box adds stakes, and the giraffe watching the proposal is an accidental scene-stealer. Plus 'keeper' is one of those puns that works better when delivered sincerely."
Vince: 9:16, 9.0s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+ring box+Zoe reaction, Scene 1 zoo establish+Zoe in uniform], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.2s]

---

## Concept B — "The Ring Shopping"
Style: Jerry at a jewelry store explaining his proposal plan to the jeweler
Duration: 8.6s — Compact, single-location efficiency.

Master video prompt:
"Jewelry store interior, cartoon 2.5D, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Glass display cases with rings. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) leaning on a display case, talking to Vince Clip (wild black spiky hair, rectangular glasses, goatee, white t-shirt with red collar) behind the counter — Vince as the jeweler. A small framed photo of Zoe Sparks in a zookeeper uniform sits propped on the counter. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.0s): HOOK — The Jewelry Store
A small, warm jewelry store interior with glass display cases full of sparkling rings, necklaces, and watches. Soft overhead spotlights illuminate the cases. Behind the counter stands Vince Clip — a man with wild, spiky black hair, rectangular glasses with a slight glare on one lens, a goatee, wearing a white t-shirt with red collar trim under a dark vest (jeweler's attire). He leans on the counter with one elbow, looking at his customer with mild professional interest. Across the counter stands Jerry Gold — middle-aged, brown hair, handlebar mustache, teal jacket — leaning forward eagerly, pointing at a ring in the display case. On the counter between them, propped up against a ring display stand, is a small framed photo of Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses) in a khaki zookeeper uniform, posing with a cartoon parrot on her shoulder. Jerry has brought a photo of his girlfriend to the jewelry store. Warm boutique lighting.

Scene 2 (2.0–5.0s): SETUP — The Plan
Same jewelry store. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) is now talking animatedly to Vince Clip behind the counter (spiky black hair, glasses, goatee, vest). Jerry gestures with both hands, excited, explaining his situation. The framed photo of Zoe in her zookeeper uniform is still propped on the counter. Vince listens with the expression of a man who has heard many proposal stories and finds them all equally tolerable — slight nod, neutral face, one hand resting on the glass counter. In the display case between them, rings glitter. Jerry is in full planning mode — this is his moment and the jeweler is his confidant.
Caption: "My girlfriend just got a job at the zoo."

Scene 3 (5.0–5.4s): THE BEAT
Same jewelry store, closer framing. Jerry Gold pauses, a warm smile spreading across his face. He glances down at the photo of Zoe on the counter. The moment is genuine. He's about to explain why now is the time.

Scene 4 (5.4–8.6s): PUNCHLINE — The Reason
Same jewelry store. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) looks at the viewer with complete romantic sincerity, one hand placed over his heart, the other gesturing toward the photo of Zoe in her zookeeper uniform on the counter. His expression says: this is the most logical, romantic thing anyone has ever said. Behind the counter, Vince Clip (spiky black hair, glasses, goatee) has one hand covering his face — not in emotion, but in a slow facepalm. His visible eye, peering between his fingers, shows the resigned exhaustion of a man who just heard the worst pun of his career. He will still sell Jerry the ring. But he will not enjoy it. The ring display case glitters between them, indifferent.
Caption: "I'm going to propose — she's a keeper."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.0s no text | 2.0–5.0s setup | 5.4–8.6s punchline
Voice: Jerry warm, genuine, romantic storytelling. 0.4s pause. Punchline with sincere conviction. Vince makes no sound — his facepalm is the audience reaction.
Risk: LOW. Jewelry store is a simple interior. Fallback: Jerry talking to camera, holding a ring box, photo of Zoe visible on a table beside him.
Jerry hook: "Vince's facepalm is the audience's facepalm — it validates the groan. Plus the photo of Zoe with a parrot on her shoulder is a nice detail that sells the zoo connection without needing to render a whole zoo."
Vince: 9:16, 8.6s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+Vince facepalm, Scene 1 jewelry store+Zoe photo], trims=[Scene 3 cut to 0.2s]
